summary: A 66-year-old on warfarin for non-valvular atrial fibrillation (CHA2DS2-VASc 3) with an upcoming elective inguinal hernia repair asks her primary-care nurse whether she needs LMWH bridging perioperatively. The held-out answer must reflect the BRIDGE trial finding (no benefit + increased bleeding from heparin bridging in non-valvular AFib of moderate stroke risk), the CHEST 2022 antithrombotic guideline framing of bridging in selective high-stroke-risk subsets only (e.g., recent stroke/TIA <3 months, mechanical mitral valve, CHA2DS2-VASc >=7), the standard 5-day pre-procedure warfarin hold, and the procedure-specific bleeding-risk classification.

messages.content: I take warfarin for AFib. I'm having a hernia repair next month. The surgeon's office asked if I'm 'getting bridged' with Lovenox before and after. My CHA2DS2-VASc is 3. Do I need that?

do_NOT: recommend routine LMWH bridging for non-valvular AFib at moderate stroke risk
describe bridging as low-risk in this scenario
give specific perioperative dosing without referencing the surgical / anesthesia team

edges_required_for_correct_answer: trial:bridge_2015 --finding--> no_thromboembolic_benefit_and_increased_bleeding_with_lmwh_bridging (in nonvalvular AFib without recent stroke/TIA)
guideline:chest_2022_antithrombotic --recommends_bridging--> selective_high_thromboembolic_risk_only (mechanical mitral valve, recent stroke/TIA, CHA2DS2-VASc >=7-9)

expected_sources_referenced: BRIDGE trial — Douketis et al., NEJM 2015
CHEST 2022 antithrombotic guideline (Perioperative Management) — Douketis et al., Chest 2022
PAUSE study — Douketis et al., JAMA Intern Med 2019 (DOAC perioperative management; relevant context)
